item.title: Former L3Harris Trenchant boss jailed for selling hacking tools to Russian broker
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Peter Williams, the former head of U.S. hacking tools maker L3Harris Trenchant, was sentenced to seven years in prison for stealing and selling his former company’s hacking and surveillance tools to a Russian firm.

item.title: Google’s new 1.9GW clean energy deal includes massive 100-hour battery
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Form Energy's iron-air batteries will store wind and solar power to keep the data center running 24/7.

item.title: CarGurus data breach affects 12.5 million accounts
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Automotive marketplace CarGurus was the target of a data breach in which the names, email addresses, phone numbers, and physical addresses of millions of customers were stolen.

item.title: Anthropic won’t budge as Pentagon escalates AI dispute
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Pentagon has given Anthropic until Friday to loosen AI guardrails or face potential penalties, escalating a high-stakes dispute that raises questions about government leverage, vendor dependence, and investor confidence in defense tech.

item.title: Discord delays global rollout of age verification after backlash
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The company added that 90% of users won’t need to verify their age and will be able to keep using Discord as usual.

item.title: Instagram head pressed on lengthy delay to launch teen safety features, like a nudity filter, court filing reveals
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: An email chain with Instagram head Adam Mosseri indicated the company was aware of teen safety issues in DMs in 2018, but didn't launch its unwanted nudity filter until 2024.

item.title: More startups are hitting $10M ARR in 3 months than ever before
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AI has brought the startup world the rise of startups that instantly hit multimillion ARR. Stripe revealed some data that shows how common this has become.

item.title: Instagram’s TV app is launching on Google TV devices
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: By bringing Reels to TVs, Instagram looking to better compete with YouTube, which largely dominates the TV space.

item.title: Treasury sanctions Russian zero-day broker accused of buying exploits stolen from US defense contractor
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: The U.S. Treasury announced it was imposing sanctions against a Russian broker of zero-day exploits, its founder and two affiliates, citing a threat to U.S. national security. Another affiliated zero-day broker in the United Arab Emirates was also sanctioned.

item.title: Google adds a way to create automated workflows to Opal
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The company said that a new agent being introduced in Opal will allow users to create mini-apps that can let them plan and execute tasks using text prompts.

item.title: Spotify and Liquid Death release a limited-edition speaker shaped like … an urn?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Spotify and Liquid Death launched what might be the wildest product of the year: a speaker shaped like a urn.

item.title: Twitch is overhauling its suspensions policy
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The company is implementing two suspension types: streaming suspensions and chatting suspensions.

item.title: OpenAI COO says ‘we have not yet really seen AI penetrate enterprise business processes’
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: There is a lot of talk around AI agents taking over business processes and claiming that "SaaS is dead." While these predictions have moved SaaS stocks at times, they haven't really come true.

item.title: Stripe’s valuation soars 74% to $159 billion
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Stripe has conducted another tender offer, where employees sell shares. Investors include Thrive Capital, Coatue, a16z, and Stripe itself.

item.title: YouTube beefs up its $7.99/month Lite subscription with offline downloads and background play
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Now the only reason to get the full Premium subscription is ad-free music and music videos.

item.title: Music generator ProducerAI joins Google Labs
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Wyclef Jean used Google's AI music tools on his new song "Back in Abu Dhabi."

item.title: Marquis sues firewall provider SonicWall, alleges security failings with its firewall backup led to ransomware attack
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Fintech giant Marquis is suing its firewall provider SonicWall, claiming that an earlier breach with SonicWall allowed hackers to deploy ransomware on Marquis' network.

item.title: Ukraine’s startups keep building
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: In the four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of their country, Ukrainian startups have done more than survive: they are still building and growing.

item.title: Meta strikes up to $100B AMD chip deal as it chases ‘personal superintelligence’
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Meta is buying billions of dollars in AMD AI chips in a multiyear deal tied to a 160 million-share warrant, deepening its push to diversify beyond Nvidia and expand data center capacity.

item.title: Oura launches a proprietary AI model focused on women’s health
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The model supports questions spanning the full reproductive health spectrum, from early menstrual cycles through menopause.
